pages.text: “Kurukukuuu! It’s my birthday, and it’s also a school day. Wake up, Zingisile! Today is your birthday!”
“Mama, Mama! Guess what day it is?” I shout. I always ask my mama every morning on my birthday.
“This is your birthday porridge. You can’t be late on Now, quickly, eat up your birthday!” and get ready.
! p u l Mmmhhh. G I love mealie porridge when G! p u l l it’s runny... u p G!
At preschool everyone knows it’s my birthday. happy birthday They sing and give me handmade love notes.

I can’t wait for my special I can’t wait to see my meal from Mama. favourite cousin, Sisipho. She is also my best friend. Her birthday is coming in September. Which chicken will Mama cook for me? I hope it’s the fat one.
“Zingisile, what was “Pumpkin and mealie pap!” your favourite part of I shout, still in my the story?” asks daydream. Mrs Mkombe. All my friends laugh.
When I get home, Luhambo is excited. He has only had one birthday, on the 28th of April. Now I’ve had five birthdays already!
Look at all the food! Mama has made my favourites – spinach and sweetcorn and pumpkin and mealie pap and gravy and her special bread. Everything is from our garden.
“Happy birthday, Zingisile! Mini emnandi kuwe, Zingisile! How old are you now?”
“I’m five years old now!” I sing back. My papa picks me up and spins me around. “My Zing-star! You’re so old you’re almost a teacher!”
Mama lets me pick the biggest piece of chicken. She dishes for me first. I dip my delicious bread in the gravy and I smile. I can’t wait for my next birthday!
